claims.claim_text: A model service can return successful responses quickly even while the model behavior has degraded.
claims.anchor_text: The API may return HTTP 200 in under 50 ms.
claims.suggested_rewrite: The API may return successful HTTP 200 responses quickly.
claims.rationale: The precise latency figure is illustrative, but the operational lesson that infrastructure health can mask model staleness is real and supported.

claims.claim_text: The war story claims the model started at 94% accuracy.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Slow Decline
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed anecdotal metric with false precision.

claims.claim_text: The war story claims the model fell to 71% accuracy after twelve months.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Slow Decline
claims.anchor_text: Twelve months later, accuracy had fallen to 71%.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Over time, the model's accuracy had degraded substantially.
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed anecdotal metric with a precise timeframe and percentage.

claims.claim_text: A large jump in positive prediction rate can indicate drift or another production issue.
claims.anchor_text: A binary classifier that normally predicts positive 2% of the time and suddenly predicts positive 20% of the time may be experiencing data drift, concept drift, a broken feature, or a legitimate external event.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A binary classifier that normally predicts positive only rarely and then suddenly predicts it much more often may be experiencing data drift, concept drift, a broken feature, or a legitimate external event.
claims.rationale: The exact percentages are illustrative, but prediction-drift monitoring as a concept is supported by the cited model-monitoring documentation.

claims.claim_text: A large change in approval rate can indicate prediction drift.
claims.anchor_text: A classifier that used to approve 30% of applications now approves 60%.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A classifier that used to approve a much smaller share of applications now approves a much larger share.
claims.rationale: The exact approval rates are illustrative, but the underlying lesson about output-distribution change is real and supported.

claims.claim_text: PSI is especially common in credit risk and regulated financial settings.
claims.span_hint: section: Drift Detection Methods and Baselines
claims.anchor_text: Population Stability Index, or PSI, is common in credit risk and regulated financial contexts.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Population Stability Index, or PSI, is widely used in credit risk monitoring and also appears in broader model-monitoring workflows.
claims.rationale: The allowlisted evidence cleanly supports widespread use in credit-risk scoring, but not the broader regulated-finance phrasing as written.

claims.claim_text: Jensen-Shannon divergence is symmetric and bounded.
claims.span_hint: section: Drift Detection Methods and Baselines
claims.rationale: The referenced page directly describes Jensen-Shannon divergence as symmetric and bounded.

claims.claim_text: Training-serving skew is one of the most common causes of production model failure.
claims.anchor_text: It is one of the most common causes of production model failure.
claims.suggested_rewrite: It is a common cause of production model failure.
claims.rationale: The superlative frequency ranking is not supported by a matching allowlisted source.

claims.claim_text: Evidently is an open-source ML monitoring framework that supports reports, test suites, and exportable outputs for automation.
claims.rationale: The README directly describes Evidently as open source and documents reports, test suites, and JSON/Python/HTML outputs.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus labels should avoid high-cardinality dimensions such as user IDs or other unbounded values.
claims.rationale: Prometheus label best-practice documentation explicitly warns against labels with high cardinality and unbounded value sets.

claims.claim_text: NannyML can estimate post-deployment model performance before targets arrive and can detect drift.
claims.rationale: The README directly states that NannyML estimates post-deployment performance without targets and detects data drift.

further_reading.title: Azure Machine Learning model monitoring
further_reading.why_relevant: A concise vendor guide to monitoring signals, reference windows, thresholds, and alerting patterns for production ML.

further_reading.title: Prometheus metric and label naming
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers the label-cardinality constraints and metric design choices that matter when exporting ML monitoring signals to Prometheus.

further_reading.title: NannyML README
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful for the delayed-label part of the module because it focuses on post-deployment performance estimation and drift analysis.

notes: Skipped Mermaid diagrams, code blocks, JSON/YAML examples, tables, quiz items, and exercise steps per seed rules; captured only prose claims and named-tool capability assertions that need inline support or rewriting.
